title: Income matters, but it is not destiny
source: World Bank Statistical Performance Indicators
visdescription: Analysis showing that while statistical performance is correlated with income, many economies outperform what would be expected of an economy at their income level.
textParagraph: Countries such as Mexico, Burkina Faso, Senegal, Uzbekistan, and the Philippines substantially overperform relative to what would be predicted by their GDP per capita, showing that institutions and sustained investments can build high-performing statistical systems even at modest income levels.
